welcomeTitle: Welcome to the vulnerability audit tool
welcomeSubtitle: Centralize client, scope, agreement, analyses, and audit results in a clearer and more professional interface.
conventionText: By signing this agreement, you permanently lock the IP/Port scope. The client and targets can no longer be modified.
signConventionNeedTarget: At least one validated IP in scope is required before signing the agreement.
noVulnerability: No vulnerability recorded for this audit.
remediationPlaceholder: Enter the remediation to apply for this vulnerability.
toastClientLocked: The client can no longer be edited after signing.
toastConventionRequiredToScan: The audit agreement must be signed before launching an analysis.
homeAuditableIntro: Auditable is your open-source pentest tool developed by Auditable.
homeHowToStart: How to get started with this tool
homeSteps: First, you cannot do anything until you have entered your client. Use the Client tab in the top menu to create and manage your client.

Once the client has been created, use the Client tab to define the audit scope through the Scan button. You must define the IPs, their ports, and the tools used for analysis. You cannot launch an analysis until the scope has been validated with the client, and you cannot add the same IP twice for the same audit.

After defining the scope, print the audit agreement. It summarizes the current context: client, auditor, analyzed assets, scans, date, audit period start/end dates, and client/auditor signature sections.
Once the agreement is printed and physically signed, validate the digital version through the Agreement button available from the Client tab.
When the audit agreement is digitally signed, the audit scope, IPs, ports, tools, and settings can no longer be modified. Check everything carefully with your client before validating.
You can now return to the Client page, open Manage Analyses, and launch the analysis from the button at the bottom of the page. The analysis duration depends on the size of the scope.
Once the analysis is complete, return to the Client tab. A View Audit button will appear so you can view the results. Results are currently displayed in English.
If you used make install_with_translategemma during installation, you can use the Translation tab to translate results automatically.
If you need to translate other content and the AI model has been installed, a Translator page will be available from the navigation bar.
In the Settings tab, you can adjust tool options while the agreement has not been signed.
settingsSubtitle: Configure aggressiveness, export formats, and tool-specific options.
nucleiRateLimitHelp: The selected aggressiveness determines the Nuclei rate-limit.
toastNucleiSeverityRequired: Select at least one Nuclei severity.
logsSubtitle: View saved logs for each tool and external source, grouped by IP.
name: Name
conventionSubtitle: Document defining the parties, scope, tools, responsibilities, and administrative security conditions of the audit.
conventionLegalTitle: Audit agreement and administrative security clauses
conventionGenericParagraphs: The parties agree as follows: this agreement is entered into by auditor {{auditorName}} and {{clientCompany}}, represented by {{clientManager}}, located at {{clientAddress}}, reachable by email at {{clientEmail}} and by phone at {{clientPhone}}, for audit number {{auditId}}.

Preamble: the purpose of the security audit is to assess the technical exposure of the audited client's information system within the scope defined below. This agreement formalizes the authorization granted to the auditor, the limits of the engagement, and the administrative responsibilities of the parties.

Definitions: Agreement means this document; Security Audit means analysis, testing, and vulnerability qualification activities; Scope means the explicitly listed IP addresses, ports, and resources; Technical Records means the data, traces, and results collected during the audit; Final Report means the deliverable provided at the end of the engagement.

Purpose: this agreement defines the audit execution conditions, authorized tools, applied settings, technical scope, and the duties of cooperation, care, and confidentiality applicable to the parties.

Duration and changes: the agreement becomes effective when signed by the parties and covers only the period required to prepare, perform, and report on the audit. Any change to the scope, ports, tools, or settings must be mutually approved before the digital signature is applied in the application.

Auditor commitments: the auditor undertakes to perform the audit strictly within the authorized scope, to use only the planned tools and settings, to limit actions to what is necessary to qualify vulnerabilities, not to perform out-of-scope actions without prior client approval, and to notify the client if a major security issue is discovered.

The auditor acts under a best-efforts obligation. The auditor takes reasonable precautions to limit disruption, but the client acknowledges that a security audit may generate logs, interact with exposed services, and, despite precautions, involve inherent technical risks.

Audited client commitments: the client states that it holds the required rights and authorizations over the systems included in scope. The client undertakes to provide accurate information about audited resources, notify the auditor of any constraint or change affecting the information system, and make a technical contact available when required.

Implementation: solely for the purposes of the audit, the client authorizes the auditor to access or attempt to access resources within scope, perform penetration tests, vulnerability scans, configuration checks, and technical actions proportionate to the audit objectives.

Operational precautions: the client remains responsible for the operation, backup, integrity, and availability of its information system. The client may request that any action presenting a risk to equipment, data, personnel, or services be interrupted; such interruption may be mentioned in the deliverables if it affects the results.

Collected data and confidentiality: information, documents, results, logs, technical records, identified vulnerabilities, and reports produced during the audit are confidential. Each party agrees to disclose them only to persons who need to know them for the performance or follow-up of the audit.

The auditor takes appropriate precautions to avoid infringing the secrecy of correspondence, personal data, or sensitive client information. The client shall identify in advance any processing activity or resource requiring specific restrictions.

Final report: at the end of the audit, the auditor provides a deliverable listing the identified vulnerabilities, their criticality when available, useful technical details, and remediation recommendations. Any limitations encountered during the audit may be stated in the report.

Liability and ownership: each party retains ownership of the hardware, software, documents, and data it makes available. No transfer of ownership occurs under this agreement. The auditor's liability may only be sought for direct damages resulting from a proven breach of obligations within the authorized scope.

End of audit and disputes: at the end of the audit, the auditor removes, where reasonably possible, any temporary tools or files deposited during the engagement, excluding normal traces kept in logs. In the event of a dispute concerning the interpretation or performance of the agreement, the parties shall first seek an amicable resolution.
conventionNoSelectedTool: No tool selected for the analysis.
conventionPhysicalSignatureNotice: The electronic signature will only be legally valid if both parties have signed the physical version of this audit agreement.
toastPdfExportError: Unable to prepare the PDF export.
toastPdfPopupBlocked: The browser blocked the PDF page. Please allow pop-ups for this application.
toastPdfFallbackOpened: The PDF page was opened using the browser fallback mode.
auditExportSubtitle: Export generated on {{date}} from the results stored in Auditable.
auditExportFooter: Document generated by Auditable. Results must be reviewed and validated by the auditor before final delivery to the client.
toastHtmlExportError: Unable to generate the HTML export.
guideNoSteps: No guideable element is visible on this page.
guideStepHeaderDescription: This area shows the application name and the signed-in user.
guideStepNavigationDescription: Use these links to access the main pages: home, client, agreement, analyses, settings, logs, and audit depending on context.
guideStepLanguageDescription: This menu changes the interface language and the frontend-generated exports.
guideHomeTitleDescription: This page explains the general workflow and the recommended order of use.
guideHomeStepsDescription: These steps explain how to create the client, prepare the scope, sign the agreement, and run the analysis.
guideClientInfoDescription: These values come from the database and are used in the agreement and audit context.
guideClientStatusDescription: This badge shows whether the agreement is signed. Once signed, the scope and some settings are locked.
guideClientActionsDescription: These buttons open the agreement, analyses, settings, logs, and audit report when results exist.
guideClientFormDescription: Enter the client's administrative information. It will be reused in the audit agreement.
guideClientEditFormDescription: You can edit client information as long as the agreement has not been signed.
guideConventionDocumentDescription: This area contains the administrative document to review with the client before signing.
guideConventionPartiesDescription: This section displays client information and the auditor name configured in the environment.
guideConventionToolsDescription: Only tools planned for the analysis are shown, with their main parameters.
guideConventionScopeDescription: This table lists IP addresses and ports declared in scope, including the true_cmd_port value stored in the database.
guideConventionActionsDescription: Export the agreement to PDF, then sign it digitally only after validating the scope and the physical version.
guideAnalysesToolsDescription: Select the tools used during the analysis. These choices also feed the agreement.
guideAnalysesScopeFormDescription: Add or edit a target and specify the ports to analyze, or use auto mode when planned.
guideAnalysesTargetsDescription: This list represents the scope that will be locked after the agreement is signed.
guideAnalysesLaunchDescription: Once the agreement is signed, run the analysis with the selected tools.
guideSettingsGridDescription: Each row configures a tool: aggressiveness, export formats, and specific options.
guideSettingsCardDescription: Edit these settings before signing the agreement. After signing, they are locked.
guideSettingsNiktoDescription: These options target specific Nikto test families and adapt the noise level of the analysis.
guideLogsFilterDescription: Filter logs by tool to find scan outputs or API responses more quickly.
guideLogsEntriesDescription: Each card is a log produced during the analysis or NVD/CIRCL enrichment.
guideLogsContentDescription: Technical content is kept here, with report paths when files were generated.
guideAuditVulnsDescription: Each vulnerability is displayed separately with badges, score, description, and recommendation.
guideAuditRemediationDescription: You can complete or edit the remediation to prepare a report usable by the client.
guideAuditExportsDescription: Export the audit report as HTML or PDF with a layout suitable for reading and sharing.
guideDefaultPageDescription: This area contains the main elements of the current page.
